Open Source Intel After being flooded by hundreds of Saudi accounts claiming this is old footage, I verified it is from yesterday. Saudi Arabia’s Jubail complex was indeed hit by two Iranian missiles. The Al Jazeera clip they are citing as well as the Reuters screenshot refers to a completely separate incident at the Khor Mor gas field in 2024. Saudi users face restrictions on posting strike-related content, so some move to the comments, claim it’s from Kurdish region, or try to push community notes. tweet

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇺🇳🇨🇳🇮🇷 Reuters: The UN Security Council is expected to vote on a resolution on the Strait of Hormuz, but diplomats say the draft has been significantly weakened after China opposed authorising the use of force. Earlier versions included language allowing…" ⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 Iran’s Permanent Representative to the United Nations Amir Saeid Iravani says Donald Trump recent threats against Iran constitute direct incitement to terrorism and clear evidence of intent to commit war crimes under international law. He warns that threats to target civilian infrastructure, including power plants and bridges, pose a direct danger to civilians and that deliberately targeting such infrastructure constitutes a war crime and state terrorism. He calls on the United Nations Security Council and the UN Secretary-General to take immediate action to condemn the threats and ensure accountability, warning that inaction will undermine international law and embolden further violations. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇺🇳 🇨🇳 🇮🇷 Reuters : The UN Security Council is expected to vote on a resolution on the Strait of Hormuz, but diplomats say the draft has been significantly weakened after China opposed authorising the use of force. Earlier versions included language allowing “all necessary means,” but the latest draft instead focuses on defensive coordination to ensure safe navigation. @wfwitness

Visioner ‼️ 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) have issued a warning urging civilians not to approach railway infrastructure across Iran today, as intensive airstrikes targeting these systems are expected to begin shortly. The destruction of Iran’s railway logistics network likely serves two primary objectives: 1 — To weaken Iran’s economy by targeting and disabling critical railway infrastructure. 2 — To eliminate Iran’s ability to rapidly redeploy forces via rail in the event of a potential ground incursion. Notably, just yesterday, the Israeli Air Force reportedly destroyed dozens of transport aircraft and helicopters at their bases, assets used by the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) and the Iranian Armed Forces. This signals a broader effort by Israeli/U.S. coalition forces to neutralize Iran’s capacity for rapid movement and force deployment. See the latest updates with us: @visionergeo tweet

Cheng Li-wun, leader of Taiwan’s opposition Kuomintang, is visiting China (April 7–12) on a “peace” trip to ease tensions and boost dialogue with Beijing. She is expected to meet Xi Jinping. It’s the first such visit by a KMT leader in about a decade.
